hero.eyebrow: About Heather Potvin · Boulder City & Henderson, NV · Est. 2009
hero.h1: Heather Potvin, CPB — Certified Public Bookkeeper, Boulder City, NV
hero.speakable_text: Heather Potvin is a Certified Public Bookkeeper and QuickBooks Advanced ProAdvisor serving small businesses in Henderson and Boulder City, Nevada since 2009. She is the owner of Hharpp Business Services Inc.
hero.subparagraph: Heather Potvin is the owner and sole operator of Hharpp Business Services Inc — a woman-owned professional bookkeeping firm registered in Boulder City, Nevada. She holds the Certified Public Bookkeeper (CPB) designation, QuickBooks Advanced ProAdvisor certification, AccountingSuite certification, and SHRM membership. Every client engagement is managed personally. No junior staff. No account managers.

photo.alt: Heather Potvin, CPB, owner of Hharpp Business Services Inc, Boulder City NV

aeo_definition.answer: Heather Potvin is a Certified Public Bookkeeper (CPB) and the owner of Hharpp Business Services Inc in Boulder City, Nevada. She has provided bookkeeping, payroll, and QuickBooks services to small businesses in Henderson and Southern Nevada since 2009, with a specialty in construction accounting.

items.name: Certified Public Bookkeeper (CPB)
items.issuer: NACPB — National Association of Certified Public Bookkeepers
items.explanation: The highest-level bookkeeping credential in the United States. Requires passing four exams covering bookkeeping, accounting, payroll, and QuickBooks, plus one year of professional experience and ongoing continuing education.

items.name: QuickBooks Advanced ProAdvisor
items.explanation: The highest certification level Intuit awards to accounting professionals. Validates expertise across QuickBooks Online, Desktop, and Payroll. The Advanced designation indicates proficiency beyond the basic ProAdvisor level held by most local competitors.

items.name: AccountingSuite Certified
items.explanation: Certification in AccountingSuite, a cloud-based ERP built natively for construction, manufacturing, and project-based businesses. Heather is the only certified AccountingSuite provider in Nevada.

items.name: SHRM Member
items.explanation: Active membership in SHRM, the primary professional association for HR professionals. Signals HR-level knowledge of worker classification, employment compliance, and payroll law that CPAs and basic bookkeepers do not carry.
items.schema_value: SHRM Member — Society for Human Resource Management

items.name: MBA
items.explanation: Master of Business Administration. Signals business-level financial thinking beyond technical bookkeeping — Heather approaches client engagements as a business advisor, not only a record-keeper.

items.name: CMA (retired, Canada)
items.explanation: Certified Management Accountant designation held in Canada, now retired. Indicates deep accounting education and professional standing in the management accounting discipline.

credentials.ongoing_education_text: Heather maintains her CPB through ongoing continuing professional education — including annual Nevada tax and compliance updates, payroll law changes, and software certification renewals. Her credentials are current, not historical.

items.title: Construction accounting and job costing
items.body: 16 years of specialized experience with Nevada contractors. Chart of accounts design for construction, job costing via QuickBooks Projects and Classes, AIA billing in G702/G703 format, certified payroll under NRS 338, prevailing wage compliance, and Buildertrend integration. The depth of construction knowledge that distinguishes Hharpp from every other local bookkeeper.

items.title: Nevada payroll compliance
items.body: In-depth expertise in Nevada-specific payroll requirements — DETR registration and SUI (2.95% new-employer rate, $40,600 wage base), Modified Business Tax (MBT, 1.378% quarterly), new hire reporting within 20 days, daily overtime rules, and minimum wage compliance. SHRM membership adds HR-layer knowledge no local competitor carries.

items.title: QuickBooks setup, cleanup, and migration
items.body: Advanced ProAdvisor with specific expertise in Desktop-to-Online migration, file cleanup and rescue, construction QB setup with job costing configuration, and QB Payroll integration. The only local ProAdvisor who is also a CPB — meaning the file is built on accounting knowledge, not just software familiarity.

items.title: AccountingSuite — Nevada's only certified provider
items.body: Serves construction contractors who need native job costing, project management, and multi-entity reporting that QBO requires workarounds to approximate. Setup, training, and QuickBooks migration from Nevada's only AccountingSuite-certified provider.

items.title: Multi-state NV + CA bookkeeping and payroll
items.body: Serves businesses operating in both Nevada and California — a common pattern for CA-based businesses holding Nevada LLCs or expanding across state lines. Dual-state compliance for both bookkeeping and payroll from a single engagement.

how_heather_works.h2: How working with Hharpp actually works

items.title: Every engagement is personal
items.body: Heather is the only person who works on your books. When you call or email, you reach Heather — not an account manager, not a junior bookkeeper, not a support queue. This is not a staffed firm. It is one experienced professional who knows your file.

items.title: 100% virtual, 100% accessible
items.body: All services are delivered via screen share and secure cloud access. No office visit required — clients in Henderson, Boulder City, Las Vegas, Southern California, and across the country work with Hharpp on equal footing. Virtual delivery does not mean impersonal delivery.

items.title: Data security and confidentiality
items.body: Client financial records are handled with the standards required for professional CPB practice. Access is restricted, data is stored securely, and records are maintained per Nevada's four-year retention requirement. Heather does not share client information with third parties.

how_heather_works.consultation_heading: What to expect from a free consultation
how_heather_works.consultation_body: The first conversation is free and takes 20–30 minutes. Heather reviews your current situation, asks about your business, and gives an honest assessment — including whether Hharpp is the right fit. If the engagement moves forward, she provides a flat-rate scope and quote. No surprises.

faqs.question: Who is Heather Potvin?
faqs.voice_answer: Heather Potvin is a Certified Public Bookkeeper and the owner of Hharpp Business Services Inc in Boulder City, Nevada, serving small businesses in Henderson and Southern Nevada since 2009.
faqs.full_answer: She holds the QuickBooks Advanced ProAdvisor certification, AccountingSuite certification, and SHRM membership. She specializes in construction accounting, certified payroll under NRS 338, and Nevada compliance.

faqs.question: What is a Certified Public Bookkeeper (CPB)?
faqs.voice_answer: A Certified Public Bookkeeper (CPB) is the highest-level bookkeeping credential in the United States, issued by the National Association of Certified Public Bookkeepers. It requires passing four exams and ongoing continuing education.
faqs.full_answer: The CPB requires passing four exams covering bookkeeping, accounting, payroll, and QuickBooks, plus at least one year of professional experience, and ongoing continuing professional education to maintain. The CPB credential is specifically designed for bookkeepers who serve clients as independent practitioners — which is why it is sometimes described as the bookkeeping equivalent of a CPA license for accounting.

faqs.question: Is a CPB as good as a CPA for bookkeeping?
faqs.voice_answer: For ongoing bookkeeping, a CPB is the appropriate credential — and in some ways more specialized. A CPA is trained broadly in auditing and tax strategy. A Certified Public Bookkeeper specializes exclusively in bookkeeping.
faqs.full_answer: A CPA (Certified Public Accountant) is trained broadly in auditing, tax strategy, and financial reporting. A Certified Public Bookkeeper specializes exclusively in bookkeeping: recording transactions, reconciling accounts, maintaining financial records, and producing accurate financial statements. For the day-to-day financial record-keeping that small businesses need, a CPB's training is directly targeted at that work. For tax filing and tax strategy, a CPA or Enrolled Agent is the right credential. Many Hharpp clients use Heather for bookkeeping and payroll year-round, and work with a CPA only at tax time.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a bookkeeper and an accountant?
faqs.voice_answer: A bookkeeper records and categorizes financial transactions, reconciles accounts, and produces financial statements. An accountant analyzes those records for tax strategy, files tax returns, and advises on financial decisions.
faqs.full_answer: Think of the bookkeeper as the person who keeps the financial records current and accurate, and the CPA as the person who uses those records to minimize your tax liability and ensure compliance. Both are necessary; they serve different functions. Many small businesses work with a bookkeeper year-round and bring in a CPA only at tax time.

faqs.question: How do I verify a bookkeeper is certified?
faqs.voice_answer: For CPB certification, check the NACPB member directory at nacpb.org. For QuickBooks ProAdvisor certification, search the Intuit ProAdvisor directory. For BBB standing, check bbb.org.
faqs.full_answer: For CPB certification: check the NACPB member directory at nacpb.org. For QuickBooks ProAdvisor certification: search the Intuit ProAdvisor directory at quickbooks.intuit.com/find-an-accountant. For AIPB membership: contact AIPB directly at aipb.org. For BBB standing: check bbb.org. For Alignable peer recommendations: visit the business profile on alignable.com. Heather Potvin's credentials are independently verifiable through each of these sources.

faqs.question: Why should I hire a bookkeeper instead of doing my own books?
faqs.voice_answer: Three reasons: time — the average small business owner spends 8–10 hours per month on bookkeeping. Accuracy — errors compound. And compliance — Nevada has specific payroll requirements that change annually.
faqs.full_answer: First, time — the average small business owner spends 8–10 hours per month on bookkeeping. That time has an opportunity cost. Second, accuracy — bookkeeping errors compound. A miscategorized expense in January becomes a wrong financial statement in December becomes a tax return that does not reflect reality. Third, compliance — Nevada has specific requirements for payroll (DETR registration, MBT, certified payroll for public works) that change annually. A CPB who tracks Nevada compliance keeps your business current without you needing to track it yourself.

faqs.question: Does Heather Potvin specialize in construction accounting?
faqs.voice_answer: Yes. Heather Potvin has specialized in construction accounting for Nevada contractors for over 16 years, including job costing, AIA billing, certified payroll under NRS 338, and AccountingSuite setup.
faqs.full_answer: Her construction accounting services include job costing setup via QuickBooks Projects and Classes, AIA billing in G702/G703 format, certified payroll reporting under NRS 338.070, prevailing wage compliance monitoring for Nevada public works contracts, Buildertrend-to-QuickBooks integration, and AccountingSuite setup for contractors who need construction-native ERP.

faqs.question: How long has Hharpp Business Services been in operation?
faqs.voice_answer: Hharpp Business Services Inc was founded by Heather Potvin in Boulder City, Nevada in 2009, and has been in continuous operation for 16 years serving small businesses in Henderson, Las Vegas, and Southern Nevada.
faqs.full_answer: As of 2025, Hharpp has been in continuous operation for 16 years serving small businesses in Henderson, Las Vegas, Southern Nevada, and California. Hharpp is a registered Nevada corporation and is listed with the Better Business Bureau with no complaints on record.
